layout_description:
Option: OPT-1
Slide description: FULL-WIDTH WORKPLAN TABLE LAYOUT. The slide presents a comprehensive project workplan as a single unified table spanning the full usable slide area. The table is structured as a 5-column grid (Workstream, Details, Owner(s), and three timeline columns: 2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, and 2027) with five major workstream rows. The layout uses a flat geometry table design (Section 4 Option C) appropriate for formal project documentation. All content is contained within a single table structure with no outer container, maximizing space for the dense tabular content. The table fills the slide from edge to edge within safe margins, with minimal padding to accommodate the high information density. Dashed separator lines between rows provide visual rhythm without consuming vertical space. The timeline header row uses a light brand-colored fill to distinguish deliverable periods from the workstream structure.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: grid
  Regions: 6
  Allocation: [0.08, 0.184, 0.184, 0.184, 0.184, 0.184]

Containers:
  C0 (FULL-WIDTH WORKPLAN TABLE): A comprehensive project workplan table built using Section 4 Option C (Flat Geometry Table) principles, adapted for a 5-column × 6-row structure (1 header row + 5 workstream rows). The table spans the full usable slide width and height, filling all available space below the title with minimal top margin.

COLUMN STRUCTURE (left to right):
1. Workstream column (15% width): Contains workstream identifiers (i. Conduct discovery, ii. Select and onboard partner, iii. Implement fund accounting platform – 3rd-party funds/BDC, iv. Implement fund accounting platform - Holdings, v. Implement fund accounting platform – non-loan investments). Each workstream name is bold, brand text color, left-aligned with small left inset. Light grey fill for all workstream cells to distinguish them as row labels.

2. Details column (20% width): Contains activity descriptions for each workstream. Multiple detail items per workstream row, each as a separate text line. Body font, regular weight, brand text color, left-aligned with small left inset. White background.

3. Owner(s) column (18% width): Lists responsible parties with role prefixes (Finance/Ops:, Tech:, TO:). Role prefixes in regular weight, names in regular weight, brand text color. Multiple owners per row separated by line breaks. Left-aligned with small left inset. White background.

4-7. Timeline columns (47% width total, distributed as: 2026 Q2 = 14%, Q3 = 11%, Q4 = 11%, 2027 = 11%): Deliverables against each workstream for each time period. Each cell contains bullet points (brand-color circle bullets, small size) with deliverable text in body font, left-aligned with small left inset. White background for all deliverable cells.

HEADER ROW TREATMENT:
- Column headers (Workstream, Details, Owner(s)) are bold, brand text color, left-aligned, white background, no filled header bar.
- Below each of these three headers: a small accent tab (brand primary color, very thin height, narrow width relative to column, top corners slightly rounded, left-aligned beneath the header text, sitting fully below the text without overlap).
- Below each accent tab: a thin solid horizontal line (brand primary color) spanning that column's width only (not crossing into adjacent columns — small white gutters between columns).
- Timeline header row (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027) spans the four rightmost columns with a unified light brand-colored fill (light tint of brand primary), white bold text centered horizontally within each period cell, vertically centered. This header row has no accent tabs — the filled background distinguishes it. A thin solid horizontal line (brand primary color) runs along the bottom edge of the timeline header row, spanning all four timeline columns.
- The text "Deliverables against each workstream" appears above the timeline header cells, centered across all four timeline columns, in a smaller font size, brand text color, regular weight.

ROW STRUCTURE:
- Five workstream rows distributed evenly across the remaining vertical space using equal flexGrow values.
- Each row's height is proportional (equal shares) to ensure all content fits without overflow.
- Within each row, all cells across the five columns share identical top and bottom edges (horizontal alignment rule B2).
- Content within each cell is top-aligned with a small top inset, allowing multi-line text to flow naturally downward.

ROW SEPARATORS:
- Thin dashed grey horizontal lines between consecutive workstream rows (not after the last row).
- Separators span the full table width from left edge to right edge.
- Positioned at the boundary between rows, sitting tight to the content (minimal space above and below on this dense slide).

FONT SIZING:
- All body text (details, owners, deliverables) uses the same font size throughout the table to maintain within-slide uniformity (Rule C6).
- Font size target: medium, optimized to fit the densest cells (Owner(s) column and deliverable cells with multiple bullet points) without overflow.
- Column headers: same size as body text or slightly larger, bold weight for hierarchy.
- Workstream labels: same size as body text, bold weight.

VISUAL TREATMENT:
- No outer container border or rounded rectangle — the table sits directly on the slide background.
- No cell borders except the header underlines and row separators described above.
- Light grey fill for the Workstream column (all cells) to visually distinguish row labels from content.
- White fill for all other cells.
- No drop shadows, glows, or 3D effects.
- Minimal padding within cells (small insets on all sides) to maximize space for content on this dense slide.

CONTENT DENSITY HANDLING:
- This slide has very high content density (5 workstreams × 3 descriptive columns × 4 timeline columns = 35 content cells, many with multi-line text and bullet lists).
- Padding is minimized to the smallest acceptable value that prevents text from touching cell edges (Rule 0.1 — dense content requires tight padding).
- Dashed separator lines provide visual rhythm and section boundaries without consuming vertical space (Rule 0.10).
- Font size is uniform across all body text to maintain professional consistency, reduced slightly if necessary to fit the densest cells without overflow (Rule C6).
- The table fills the full available slide height, distributed evenly across the five workstream rows, ensuring no large empty zone appears at the bottom (Rule 0.3 — vertical rhythm).
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BACKGROUND LAYER): INACTIVE. The slide content (full-width workplan table) fills the available space completely. No decorative background layer is needed — adding one would violate Rule D1 (bottom layer used only when content does not fill the slide). The table's light grey fill in the Workstream column and the timeline header's light brand fill provide sufficient visual structure without requiring an additional background layer.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER): INACTIVE. This slide contains a single unified table structure with no cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements. All visual relationships (workstream → details → owners → timeline deliverables) are expressed through the table's row structure and do not require top-layer connector elements.

_reference_violations.description: Multiple deliverable cells in the timeline columns (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027) exhibit visible text overlap and rendering issues. Text from different deliverable items appears to be layered on top of each other, making content illegible. This is particularly severe in the 2026 Q2 column where multiple bullet points are stacked and overlapping, and in the 2027 column where text is densely packed and overlapping.

Cell content placement errors detected. In the sketch, several cells show transposed or misaligned content compared to the source slide. For example, in workstream row i (Conduct discovery), the deliverables in the timeline columns do not match the correct column positions from the source. The 2026 Q2 column in the sketch shows overlapping text that suggests multiple deliverables have been incorrectly placed or rendered in the same space.

The timeline deliverable cells (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027) are too small to accommodate the multi-line bullet point content without severe overlap and illegibility. The current column widths and row heights do not provide sufficient space for the dense deliverable text, resulting in text stacking on top of itself rather than flowing naturally.

Text rendering quality issues throughout the table make it difficult to verify complete content fidelity. Many cells show text that is either overlapping, clipped, or illegible due to rendering problems. This affects the ability to confirm that all bullet points and text content from the source slide are accurately represented.

_reference_critique:
C0 (FULL-WIDTH WORKPLAN TABLE): CRITICAL violations detected in timeline deliverable columns (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027).

1. OVERLAP VIOLATION: Text content within individual deliverable cells is overlapping and stacking on top of itself, making content illegible. This is most severe in the 2026 Q2 column and 2027 column where multiple bullet points appear to be rendered in the same vertical space rather than flowing sequentially.

2. CELL CONTENT PLACEMENT ERROR: Deliverable content does not match the correct column/row positions from the source slide. The sketch shows misaligned or transposed deliverables, particularly in the timeline columns.

3. FIT FEASIBILITY FAILURE: The timeline columns (collectively 47% width distributed as 14%, 11%, 11%, 11%) are too narrow to accommodate the multi-line bullet point content. Each deliverable cell contains 2-4 bullet points with substantial text, but the current dimensions force severe text overlap.

SVGSketcher must:
- INCREASE the width allocation for timeline columns from current 47% total to at least 55% total (redistribute as: 2026 Q2 = 16%, Q3 = 13%, Q4 = 13%, 2027 = 13%).
- REDUCE the Workstream column from 15% to 12% and Details column from 20% to 18% to compensate.
- INCREASE row heights for all five workstream rows to provide sufficient vertical space for multi-line deliverable content. Each row should be tall enough that 3-4 bullet points with wrapped text can render without overlap.
- REDUCE body font size from current value to 9pt or 10pt maximum to fit the dense deliverable content within the available cell dimensions without overlap.
- ENSURE each bullet point in deliverable cells renders on its own line with proper vertical spacing (line-height = 1.3-1.4× font size) so text does not stack.
- VERIFY that all deliverable content from the source slide is placed in the correct column AND correct row — no transposition of cells between columns or rows.
- APPLY uniform font size (9-10pt) to all body text (Details, Owner(s), and Deliverables) to maintain within-slide uniformity per Rule C6.
